key_reasons: Paired breast-primary C/N contrast outperformed baselines and remained the strongest validated option after uncertainty propagation with an explicit indeterminate rule.

lead_model: Co-leading: R2 C direct breast C/N/H + paired contrasts; R2 S2 staged hierarchical R1 C then breast C/N/H NZ-vs-AU
key_reasons: Two Rung 2 candidates now lead the field. R2 C remains the strongest direct high-information model, while R2 S2 stays competitive after uncertainty propagation, avoids direct dependence on primary H, and is easier to explain biologically; both still have high indeterminate rates and neither is operational.
Rung 3 still lacks enough support for operational use because class counts are small, deployment-relevant blocking is weak, and no validated uncertainty-aware win has been shown.
